project_overview.project_name: Development Response to Displacement Impacts Project in the Horn of Africa Phase II
project_overview.project_development_objective: To improve access to basic social and economic services, expand livelihood opportunities and enhance environmental management for host communities and refugees in the target areas
project_overview.beneficiaries: Approximately 2.5 million people (1.76 million host community members and 740,000 refugees), of whom at least 50 percent will be female

geographic_scope.fragile_or_conflict_affected_areas: yes - project operates in six main refugee-hosting regions, with specific arrangements for High Risk of Ongoing Conflict (HROC) areas including all Tigray Region woredas

key_components.component_name: Social and economic services and infrastructure
key_components.description: Improve access to basic social services and economic infrastructure through Community Investment Fund and Strategic Investment Fund subprojects, capacity building, women's leadership and voice, and social cohesion activities
Integrated natural resource management and alternative energy sources to address environmental degradation and provide renewable energy access
Traditional and non-traditional livelihood support and small-scale irrigation schemes to create economic opportunities for refugees and host communities
Project coordination, monitoring and evaluation, and policy dialogue on development responses to forced displacement
Emergency response component to address eligible crises in project areas

implementation_arrangements.implementing_agency: Ministry of Agriculture (MoA) as lead agency, with Refugees and Returnees Service (RRS) for refugee coordination
implementation_arrangements.coordination_mechanisms: Federal, Regional and Woreda Steering and Technical Committees; inter-agency coordination through MoU between MoA and RRS; collaboration with UNHCR
implementation_arrangements.procurement_approach: World Bank Procurement Regulations for IPF; four-level procurement (federal, regional, woreda, community participation); third-party implementation in HROC areas
implementation_arrangements.monitoring_and_evaluation: Results Monitoring, Evaluation and Learning system with remote monitoring tools (GEMS, IBM), third-party monitoring in HROC areas, baseline/midterm/endline evaluations

identified_risks.risk_description: Violent conflicts in different parts of the country, security challenges and risks from non-state armed groups, potential damage to infrastructure and forced displacement
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Third-party implementing entity in HROC areas, Third-Party Monitoring Agent, Security Risk Assessment and Management Plan, legal covenants for timely contracting

identified_risks.risk_description: Economic imbalances, merchandise exports decline, parallel market premium on exchange rate, ongoing conflict affecting agricultural production and fiscal position, inflation surge
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Focus on small-scale investments and local resources to address supply chain disruptions and import dependencies

identified_risks.risk_description: Capacity gaps in procurement, FM and ESRM; government officials in new areas require capacity building; unfamiliarity with World Bank frameworks
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Training and mentoring for all personnel on World Bank procurement framework and ESF; augmented technical expertise in PCU for SSI subprojects

identified_risks.risk_description: Delay in planning and low absorption capacity, turnover and capacity limitations, inadequate internal audit oversight, low-quality reporting, risk of double-dipping during parallel implementation
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Strengthening planning and internal control processes, adequate staffing, budget for internal audit review, capacity building, clear delineation of funds flow during overlap period

identified_risks.risk_description: Contextual factors of insecurity and potential tension between refugees and host communities, involuntary resettlement, labor standards compliance, GBV/SEA/SH risks, social exclusion
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: E&S instruments with mitigating measures, E&S specialists at federal and regional levels, World Bank training on ESF, expanded GRM, standalone sub-component on social cohesion, SRAMP

identified_risks.risk_description: Armed robbery, theft, insecure road transportation, GBV and SEA/SH, inter-ethnic conflict and inter-community violence
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Security Risk Assessment and Management Plan, continuous awareness and training, security focal points, collaboration with government security forces, incident response protocols

triggered_safeguard_policies: ESS1: Assessment and Management of Environmental and Social Risks and Impacts
ESS3: Resource Efficiency and Pollution Prevention and Management
ESS5: Land Acquisition, Restrictions on Land Use and Involuntary Resettlement
ESS6: Biodiversity Conservation and Sustainable Management of Living Natural Resources
ESS7: Indigenous Peoples/Sub-Saharan African Historically Underserved Traditional Local Communities
safeguards_and_social.grievance_redress_mechanism: yes - project has existing GRM that will be expanded to new project areas including refugee camps and sites, with multiple uptake channels and timelines for resolution from kebele to federal levels
safeguards_and_social.gender_considerations: Enhanced focus on women's economic and social empowerment with 45% female beneficiaries target for livelihood activities, 40% women's representation in community committees, 30% women in leadership positions, tailored leadership program for 12,000 women, GBV prevention activities

fcv_relevant_details.conflict_sensitivity: Project includes differentiated implementation arrangements for High Risk of Ongoing Conflict (HROC) vs Non-High Risk of Ongoing Conflict (NROC) areas based on Woreda Needs, Conflict and Capacity Assessment; third-party implementation in HROC areas; social cohesion sub-component to address refugee-host tensions
fcv_relevant_details.fragility_context: Ethiopia faces violent conflict in multiple regions including Northern Ethiopian Crisis, political competition along ethnic lines, climate change as threat multiplier, over 4 million IDPs with 50% displaced in past six months, destruction of public facilities in conflict areas
fcv_relevant_details.security_considerations: Security Risk Assessment and Management Plan prepared; security focal points at federal and regional levels; training for security forces on use of force principles; physical security measures at project sites; incident response and rapid demobilization plans for HROC areas
fcv_relevant_details.displacement_or_refugees: yes - Ethiopia hosts over 850,000 refugees (third-largest in Africa), mostly from South Sudan, Somalia and Eritrea; project directly benefits 740,000 refugees; addresses protracted displacement in camps; includes support for new refugee site in Amhara; over 4 million IDPs due to recent conflicts
